question_text: Which of the following best explains why atmospheric pressure decreases as you go higher up a mountain?

options.label: A
options.text: The temperature is always lower at high altitude

options.label: B
options.text: There is less air (fewer gas particles) above you at higher altitude

options.label: C
options.text: The Earth's gravity is weaker at high altitude

options.label: D
options.text: The air becomes less dense because it expands

explanation: Atmospheric pressure is caused by the weight of air above pushing down. At higher altitude, there is less air above you, so fewer particles are pressing down, resulting in lower pressure.
